description: A struct field typed as value data does not fire E-STRUCT-FUNCTION-FIELD (§14.3). With `cb: string`, every field is serializable value data, so the function-field reject stays silent. The clean twin of e-struct-function-field-pos.
rationale: §14.3 a value-typed struct field (string, number, enum, nested struct) is well-formed; E-STRUCT-FUNCTION-FIELD fires only on a function-typed field.
